Title: User Creation and Authentication
Test Data:
- User details: id=1, nome="John", cognome="Doe", email="john.doe@example.com", photo="http://example.com/photo.jpg"
- Valid credentials: username="john.doe", password="password123"
- Invalid credentials: username="invalid.user", password="wrongpassword"
Test Steps:
1. Call UserCreateProcedure with valid user details.
2. Call StoredDataDB with "Valid credentials" for authorization.
3. Call UserCreateProcedure with invalid user details.
4. Call StoredDataDB with "Not exist" for authentication.
Expected Result:
1. UserCreateProcedure should create a new user entry in StoredDataDB.
2. StoredDataDB should return authorization success.
3. UserCreateProcedure should handle invalid user details gracefully.
4. StoredDataDB should return authentication success.

Title: Creating and Viewing Posts
Test Data:
- Post content: "Hello, world!"
- User id: 1
- Neighborhood id: 1
Test Steps:
1. Create a new Post with the given content and user.
2. Store the Post in the database.
3. Retrieve the Post from the database.
4. Verify the Post content and user association.
Expected Result:
1. Post should be created successfully.
2. Post should be stored in the database.
3. Retrieved Post should match the original content and user association.

Title: Adding and Retrieving Comments
Test Data:
- Comment content: "Great post!"
- User id: 1
- Post id: 1
Test Steps:
1. Create a new Comment with the given content and user.
2. Associate the Comment with the Post.
3. Store the Comment in the database.
4. Retrieve the Comment from the database.
5. Verify the Comment content and user association.
Expected Result:
1. Comment should be created successfully.
2. Comment should be associated with the Post.
3. Comment should be stored in the database.
4. Retrieved Comment should match the original content and user association.

Title: Creating and Managing Chats
Test Steps:
1. Create a new ChatList for the given users.
2. Add the ChattedWith user to the ChatList.
3. Store the ChatList in the database.
4. Retrieve the ChatList from the database.
5. Verify the ChatList contains both users.
Expected Result:
1. ChatList should be created successfully.
2. ChattedWith user should be added to the ChatList.
3. ChatList should be stored in the database.
4. Retrieved ChatList should contain both users.

Title: Associating Posts with Neighborhoods
Preconditions: Post and Neighborhood exist in the system.
Test Steps:
1. Associate the Post with the Neighborhood.
2. Store the association in the database.
3. Retrieve the association from the database.
4. Verify the association between Post and Neighborhood.
Expected Result:
1. Association should be created successfully.
2. Association should be stored in the database.
3. Retrieved association should match the original Post and Neighborhood.

Title: Navigating to Profile Sections
Test Steps:
1. Click on the profile photo from the Home screen.
2. Verify the transition to the Profile section.
3. Click on the notifications icon from the Home screen.
4. Verify the transition to the Notification section.
5. Click on the map icon from the Home screen.
6. Verify the transition to the MapFullScreenAPI.
7. Click on the home access icon from the Home screen.
8. Verify the transition back to the Home screen.
9. Click on the personal profile access icon from the Home screen.
10. Verify the transition to the Profile section.
11. Click on the chat access icon from the Home screen.
12. Verify the transition to the Chat section.
13. Click on the general archive access icon from the Home screen.
14. Verify the transition to the GeneralDashboard.
Expected Result:
1. Transition to the Profile section should occur.
2. Transition to the Notification section should occur.
3. Transition to the MapFullScreenAPI should occur.
4. Transition back to the Home screen should occur.
5. Transition to the Profile section should occur.
6. Transition to the Chat section should occur.
7. Transition to the GeneralDashboard should occur.

Title: Browsing and Commenting on Posts
Preconditions: Post and User exist in the system.
Test Data:
- Post id: 1
- User id: 1
- Comment content: "Great post!"
Test Steps:
1. Browse the shared posts from the GeneralDashboard.
2. Click on a specific post.
3. Load the comments for the selected post.
4. Navigate to the FullScreenPost view.
5. Add a comment to the post.
6. Submit the comment.
7. Wait for the comment to be saved.
Expected Result:
1. Shared posts should be browsed successfully.
2. Specific post should be clicked and comments loaded.
3. FullScreenPost view should display the post.
4. Comment should be added successfully.
5. Comment should be submitted.
6. Comment should be saved successfully.

Title: Viewing and Interacting with Profiles
Test Steps:
1. View the resident profile from the ItemResultSearch.
2. Add the resident to contacts.
3. View the business profile from the ItemResultSearch.
4. View unsold items from the business profile.
5. View promotions from the business profile.
6. Make an appointment from the business profile.
7. Perform a database write operation for the appointment.
8. Confirm the successful operation.
Expected Result:
1. Resident profile should be viewed successfully.
2. Resident should be added to contacts.
3. Business profile should be viewed successfully.
4. Unsold items should be viewed successfully.
5. Promotions should be viewed successfully.
6. Appointment should be made successfully.
7. Database write operation should succeed.
8. Successful operation confirmation should be received.

Title: Handling Authentication Failure
Preconditions: User does not exist in the system.
Test Data:
- User details: id=1, nome="John", cognome="Doe", email="john.doe@example.com", photo="http://example.com/photo.jpg"
- Invalid credentials: username="john.doe", password="wrongpassword"
Test Steps:
1. Attempt to create a new user with invalid credentials.
2. Verify the authentication failure.
Expected Result:
1. User creation should fail due to invalid credentials.
2. Authentication failure should be handled gracefully.

Title: Handling Database Write Operation Failure
Test Steps:
1. Perform a database write operation for the appointment.
2. Verify the failure of the database write operation.
Expected Result:
1. Database write operation should fail.
2. Failure should be handled gracefully.
